questions.q: From the following information, prepare a Cash Flow Statement for the year ending 31st March, 2022:
Which of the following is not a primary purpose of a Cash Flow Statement?
questions.answer: To compare the performance of a company with its competitors.

questions.q: State the importance of a Cash Flow Statement in the decision-making process of an investor.
questions.answer: A Cash Flow Statement helps an investor to assess the liquidity of a company and its ability to meet its short-term obligations.

questions.q: What is the difference between Operating Activities and Investing Activities?
questions.answer: Operating Activities are those activities that result in the generation and collection of cash and cash equivalents in the course of the normal operating cycle of the business, whereas Investing Activities result in the acquisition and disposal of long-term assets.

questions.q: Which of the following is a non-cash item?
State the main difference between a Cash Flow Statement and a Balance Sheet.
questions.answer: A Cash Flow Statement presents the inflows and outflows of cash and cash equivalents of a company over a specific period of time, whereas a Balance Sheet presents the financial position of a company at a particular point of time.

questions.q: What is the significance of Cash and Cash Equivalents in the Cash Flow Statement?
questions.answer: Cash and Cash Equivalents are the most liquid assets of a company and are used to measure the liquidity and solvency of the company.

questions.q: Which of the following is a source of cash in the Cash Flow Statement?
Explain the purpose of the Direct Method of preparing a Cash Flow Statement.
questions.answer: The purpose of the Direct Method is to present the inflows and outflows of cash and cash equivalents on a direct basis, i.e., by showing the cash inflows and outflows separately.

questions.q: State the difference between the Direct Method and the Indirect Method of preparing a Cash Flow Statement.
questions.answer: The Direct Method presents the cash inflows and outflows separately, whereas the Indirect Method presents the cash inflows and outflows by adjusting the net profit or loss for non-cash items.
